title: atomic-skills Lessons file frontmatter (schemaVersion 0.2)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter of a per-initiative lessons file at `.atomic-skills/projects/<project-id>/<plan-slug>/lessons/<initiative-slug>.md` (Spec 2 — Phase-end lessons consolidation / G1). One file per initiative (the unit that produces work); it survives phase archival and keeps plan.md lean. Lessons are DRAFTED at phase-done from real failure signals (review-code findings, reopened/blocked tasks, deferred gates, the diff, user corrections), RATIFIED by the user, and DISPOSITIONED at the next phase's start (Apply/Keep/Stale/Reject). NOT bundled into the aiDeck consumer schema — this is a validate-state-only entity (aiDeck does not render lessons; the deferred burndown stats are list-lessons.js --stats, not a dashboard data-source).

slug.description: The initiative slug this lessons file belongs to (anti-collision key, inherited from `new initiative`'s duplicate-abort). The file is named `<slug>.md`.

projectId.description: The nested project id (top level under projects/).

parentPlan.description: The plan whose phase produced these lessons.

lesson.description: One distilled lesson. Actionable = locus + corrective action (Self-Refine): `statement` names what went wrong, `corrective` names the locus + the fix to apply next time. `scope: reusable` lessons are the only ones the phase-start gate surfaces; `local` lessons stay with the phase for the record. `confidence` is the ExpeL vote (born 2, decremented toward 0; a lesson at 0 is closed). `appliesTo` forward-refs anchor on phaseId (it exists at plan-authoring time; the future initiative's slug does not).

statement.description: What went wrong / the lesson, one sentence. Blameless.

corrective.description: Locus + corrective action: where it bit and what to do next time (Self-Refine actionable shape).

scope.description: reusable = applies to future phases (surfaced by the phase-start gate); local = specific to this phase, kept for the record only.

appliesTo.description: phaseId forward-refs this lesson should be reviewed before. [] = all future phases. split-phase rewrites these refs.

status.description: open = still actionable; closed = applied/superseded/stale (see staleReason).

owner.description: Who owns closing the lesson (Google-SRE tracked-closure). Usually the user.

evidence.description: Link/path to the source phase's self-review or the review-code finding that produced the lesson (verify-before-store).

confidence.description: ExpeL curation vote: born 2, upvoted on re-confirmation, decremented on rejection; a lesson at 0 is removed/closed.

validatedAt.description: Last time the lesson was re-checked at a phase-start gate (CUPMem stale-check stamp). Initialized to createdAt.

recurrenceOf.description: id of a prior lesson this one rhymes with — high recurrence = learning is not sticking (the user's success signal made visible).

staleReason.description: Required-when-closed-as-stale (CUPMem write-time adjudication): why the premise no longer holds.
